diagnoses.name: Influenza
diagnoses.reason: Abrupt-onset fever, dry cough, myalgia and headache in an adult during respiratory-virus season match the classical influenza prodrome described in WHO ICD-11 1E32 and NICE NG200.

treatments.notes: Most effective within 48h of symptom onset.

diagnoses.name: Community-acquired pneumonia
diagnoses.reason: Productive cough and fever raise the possibility of bacterial pneumonia; chest examination and imaging are needed before antibiotic choice.

diagnoses.name: Acute pharyngitis
diagnoses.reason: Sore throat with fever may be viral or streptococcal; risk-stratify with Centor / FeverPAIN before considering antibiotics.

disclaimer: This is a decision-support tool. Final decisions must be made by a licensed physician. Do not use as a substitute for clinical judgement, examination, or established protocols.
